statement.p: An astronaut on the moon makes a running jump off a cliff onto a broad flat plain. The clifftop is 4 m higher than the plain. Because of the slight upward jump, the astronauts height from the plain, in meters, t seconds after the jump is {-0.81t^{2}+1.3t+6}\text{.} How long will it be until the astronaut lands in the plain?

solution.p: When the astronaut lands, the height will be 0\text{.} So we must solve the equation {-0.81t^{2}+1.3t+6}=0\text{.} Using the quadratic formula:
Only the solution t\approx{3.63996} makes sense in this situation. So it will takes {3.63996\ {\rm s}} for the astronaut to land.
